PageContent.title: PageContent
PageContent.description: Represents the content for static pages such as 'About Us', 'Services', or sections of the home page, supporting multiple languages and SEO metadata.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PageContent entity.

slug.description: A URL-friendly unique identifier for the page content, e.g., 'about-us' or 'services'.

titleEn.description: The title of the page content in English.

titleUr.description: The title of the page content in Urdu.

contentEn.description: The main body content of the page in English. This can be extensive HTML or Markdown.

contentUr.description: The main body content of the page in Urdu. This can be extensive HTML or Markdown.

metaTitleEn.description: The SEO meta title for the page content in English, used by search engines.

metaTitleUr.description: The SEO meta title for the page content in Urdu, used by search engines.

metaDescriptionEn.description: The SEO meta description for the page content in English, providing a summary for search results.

metaDescriptionUr.description: The SEO meta description for the page content in Urdu, providing a summary for search results.

lastUpdated.description: Timestamp indicating when the page content was last updated.

GalleryItem.title: GalleryItem
GalleryItem.description: Represents an individual item or project showcased in the interactive gallery, including image details and multilingual descriptions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GalleryItem entity.

titleEn.description: The title of the gallery item in English.

titleUr.description: The title of the gallery item in Urdu.

descriptionEn.description: A detailed description of the gallery item or project in English.

descriptionUr.description: A detailed description of the gallery item or project in Urdu.

imageUrl.description: The URL to the high-resolution image of the gallery item.

thumbnailUrl.description: The URL to a smaller thumbnail image for the gallery item, used for previews.

order.description: An integer representing the display order of the gallery item within the gallery.

metaTitleEn.description: The SEO meta title for the gallery item in English.

metaTitleUr.description: The SEO meta title for the gallery item in Urdu.

metaDescriptionEn.description: The SEO meta description for the gallery item in English.

metaDescriptionUr.description: The SEO meta description for the gallery item in Urdu.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the gallery item was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the gallery item was last updated.

ContactSubmission.title: ContactSubmission
ContactSubmission.description: Records messages submitted by users through the website's contact form.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ContactSubmission entity.

senderName.description: The full name of the person who submitted the contact form.

senderEmail.description: The email address of the person who submitted the contact form.

subject.description: The subject line of the message from the contact form.

messageBody.description: The main content of the message submitted through the contact form.

submissionDateTime.description: Timestamp indicating when the contact message was submitted.

isRead.description: A flag indicating whether the submitted contact message has been marked as read by an administrator.

definition.description: Publicly readable content for static pages (e.g., 'About Us', 'Services'). Write access is restricted to administrators only. Includes denormalized data for SEO and multilingual support.

params.name: pageContentSlug
params.description: The URL-friendly unique identifier (slug) of the page content, used as the document ID.

definition.description: Publicly readable items showcasing past work in the interactive gallery. Write access is restricted to administrators only.

params.name: galleryItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for a gallery item, used as the document ID.

definition.description: Stores messages submitted via the contact form. Any user (including anonymous) can create submissions. Only administrators can read, update, or delete submissions.

params.name: submissionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a contact form submission, used as the document ID.

definition.description: A collection for Database Access Control (DBAC). Each document in this collection represents a user with administrative privileges. The existence of a document `admins/{uid}` confirms the `uid` is an administrator. This enables secure, server-side role checks without custom claims. (Note: 'Admin' is a conceptual entity for role management, assuming its schema, often an empty document, is implicitly defined for DBAC purposes as per mandate.)

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique Firebase Authentication UID of the administrative user, used as the document ID.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for Legacy Stone prioritizes security, scalability, and debuggability by strictly adhering to the core design principles and strategy mandates. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL) & DBAC:**
To achieve authorization independence and implement Database Access Control (DBAC) without relying on custom claims or complex hierarchical dependencies, a dedicated top-level collection, `/admins/{adminId}`, has been introduced. This collection stores the `uid`s of administrative users. For any operation requiring admin privileges (e.g., creating/updating/deleting `PageContent` or `GalleryItem`, or reading/updating/deleting `ContactSubmission`), security rules will perform a simple `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/admins/$(request.auth.uid))` check. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls to parent documents or external authorization contexts, ensuring atomic operations and simplified rule logic.

**Structural Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture):**

*   **Public Content:** `PageContent` and `GalleryItem` are stored in top-level collections (`/pageContents/{pageContentSlug}` and `/galleryItems/{galleryItemId}`). Both are designed for public read access by any user (authenticated or unauthenticated). This segregation ensures all documents within these collections share the same security posture (publicly readable), simplifying security rules to `allow read: if true;`. Write access to these collections is restricted to administrators via the `/admins` collection.
*   **User Submissions:** `ContactSubmission` (`/contactSubmissions/{submissionId}`) is a top-level collection where any user can `create` a new submission. Read, update, and delete operations are strictly limited to administrators. This separation of concerns ensures that public write access for new submissions does not compromise the privacy and management of existing submissions, which are admin-only.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**

*   For `PageContent` and `GalleryItem`, `list` operations are implicitly secured for public access, as all documents in these collections are intended for public display. Security rules will simply allow reads, and clients can fetch the entire collection or specific documents based on queries without needing client-side filtering for authorization.
*   For `ContactSubmission`, `list` operations are explicitly allowed for administrators. An admin user can securely query and retrieve all contact submissions, as the security rules will ensure that only an authenticated admin (whose `uid` exists in `/admins`) can perform these read operations. This design avoids the pitfalls of rules acting as filters, providing predictable query results based on the caller's authorization.

**Data Clarity and Predictability:**
Semantic naming is consistently used for paths and parameters (e.g., `{pageContentSlug}`, `{galleryItemId}`, `{submissionId}`, `{adminId}`). All authorization-related checks are explicit (e.g., `exists()` for admin roles). The structure promotes clear intent for data access and management, facilitating easier debugging and maintenance of security rules.
